title: F-005 HUD/SessionStart 换皮 (badge 作用域 + section + tree, parity-safe) + NO_COLOR snapshot
description: Reskin the SessionStart HUD AI sink (renderAiSink in packages/cli/templates/hooks/knowledge-hint-broad.cjs) from flat indented `[type] (maturity):` lists to the W3-B look per mockups.md#3: a section-bar title with active/reference counts (▌ Fabric Knowledge · N active · M reference), then ALWAYS-ACTIVE and REFERENCE groups rendered with scope badges ([team]/[project]/[personal]) + plain tree indent. Because the HUD renders via the .cjs hook, it may use ONLY the parity-trivial shared primitives (sectionBar + scopeBadge from lib/theme.cjs, added in TASK-001) plus a minimal plain indent — NOT the complex CLI-only tree/grid. Export renderAiSink in module.exports and ship a NO_COLOR snapshot test. theme-parity MUST stay green.
action: In knowledge-hint-broad.cjs renderAiSink: (1) replace the `[fabric:SessionStart] <store>` + 'ALWAYS-ACTIVE RULES (...)' / 'REFERENCE (...)' flat headers with sectionBar(`Fabric Knowledge · ${bodies.length} active · ${referenceEntries.length} reference`) from require('./lib/theme.cjs'); (2) sub-group labels 'ALWAYS-ACTIVE' / 'REFERENCE' as plain section sub-lines; (3) each entry line prefixed with scopeBadge(layerToScope(entry)) + plain two-space tree indent ('├─ '/'└─ ' optional but only if a minimal branch helper is byte-mirrored into theme.cjs — default plain indent to keep parity surface small) + type + id + summary. Map layer→scope: team→'team', project→'project', personal→'personal'. Use ONLY lib/theme.cjs primitives (sectionBar/scopeBadge/paint/symbol), NEVER ../tui/structure.js. Add renderAiSink to module.exports. Keep the footer/scope lines and the lang (zh/en) branching intact.

criteria: knowledge-hint-broad.cjs requires sectionBar/scopeBadge from ./lib/theme.cjs (grep 'theme.cjs' + 'sectionBar' present in the hook)
renderAiSink no longer emits the literal 'ALWAYS-ACTIVE RULES (' header line verbatim as the only header (sectionBar('Fabric Knowledge') present; grep 'Fabric Knowledge' hits)
renderAiSink uses scopeBadge for entry lines (grep 'scopeBadge' in the hook returns >=1)
the hook does NOT import ../tui/structure.js or any CLI-only complex primitive (grep -c 'tui/structure' returns 0)
renderAiSink is in module.exports (grep 'renderAiSink' inside the module.exports block)
a NO_COLOR snapshot test for renderAiSink over fixture entries/alwaysBodies exists and passes (toMatchSnapshot, NO_COLOR=1)
convergence.verification: pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test -- theme-parity && pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test -- knowledge-hint && pnpm -r exec tsc --noEmit
convergence.definition_of_done: The SessionStart HUD renders section-bar title + counts + scope-badge tree groups per mockup #3 using only parity-safe shared primitives; theme-parity stays byte-green; the HUD output is snapshot-pinned for NO_COLOR.

files.change: Reskin renderAiSink to sectionBar title + counts + scope-badge + plain tree groups using lib/theme.cjs primitives only; export renderAiSink.
require renderAiSink, snapshot output over fixture {entries, alwaysBodies, storeLabel, lang} under NO_COLOR=1.

implementation: Read renderAiSink fully (lines ~924-1019) and lib/theme.cjs exports (sectionBar/scopeBadge added in TASK-001).
Replace the store-label header + section headers with sectionBar('Fabric Knowledge · N active · M reference').
Render each always-active + reference entry with scopeBadge(scope) + plain indent + type + id + summary, preserving truncateSummary + backstop fold + zh/en branches + footer/scope lines.
Add hud-reskin.test.ts requiring the hook and snapshotting renderAiSink under NO_COLOR=1.
Run theme-parity + hook tests + tsc; commit (incremental).

reference.pattern: lib/theme.cjs byte-mirror primitives; existing renderAiSink as a pure fn; establish a NEW vitest toMatchSnapshot test with NO_COLOR=1 (grouped-help.test.ts uses toContain/toMatch, not toMatchSnapshot, and does not pin NO_COLOR — do not reuse it; add a fresh NO_COLOR=1 snapshot test)

rationale.chosen_approach: HUD renders through the .cjs hook, so per C-003 it may only use parity-trivial shared primitives (sectionBar/scopeBadge mirrored in TASK-001) plus plain indent — keeping the byte-parity surface minimal. The complex CLI-only tree/grid are deliberately NOT used here.
decision_factors: C-003 HUD-shared layer must stay parity-safe
renderAiSink is already a pure fn (just not exported)
rationale.tradeoffs: HUD tree is plain-indent (not ├─└) by default to avoid growing theme.cjs; mockup #3's ├─ is approximated unless a minimal branch helper is judged parity-trivial and mirrored.

risks: Reaching for tui/structure.js in the .cjs hook would break the runtime (ESM import in CJS) and C-003 — guarded by a grep criterion.
Any new glyph used in the HUD must be parity-mirrored or parity breaks — covered by the theme-parity gate.
